title: Kanelbullar, Swedish cardamom-cinnamon buns
subtitle: Knotted yeasted buns with cardamom in the dough
excerpt: Swedish kanelbullar in the household tradition: a soft yeasted dough scented with ground cardamom, rolled out, brushed with cinnamon butter, knotted in the Swedish style, and finished with pearl sugar after the bake. Sixteen buns.
sourceNotes: Hagdahl, Kok-konsten som vetenskap och konst (1879, public domain in Sweden): documents the Swedish bullar tradition with cardamom-scented yeasted dough. Pre-1928 Swedish household manuals document kanelbullar as the canonical fika bake. The knot shape (a strip of dough wound around the fingers and tucked) is the modern bakery form that developed from the simpler rolled-and-sliced version.

content.text: Kanelbullar are Swedish cinnamon-cardamom buns. They are eaten with coffee at fika, the daily Swedish coffee break. Two things set them apart from American cinnamon rolls. First, the dough has ground cardamom in it, so the spice runs through the whole bun, not just the filling. Second, the shape is a knot. You cut filled dough into strips, twist each strip along its length, and wind it into a knot. American rolls are rolled up and sliced into spirals.

Grind cardamom from pods rather than buying it ready-ground. The oils that give cardamom its scent fade fast once the seeds are cracked. Pearl sugar, a Swedish nibbed sugar, goes on top after baking for the snowy speckled finish. Demerara works as a substitute.

items.name: Green cardamom
items.prepNote: pods, seeds extracted and ground; about 2 teaspoons ground

items.name: Demerara sugar
items.prepNote: or pearl sugar if you can find it, for the top

content.text: Mix the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, ground {{cardamom-green}} and {{yeast-fast-action}} in a stand-mixer bowl. Add the lukewarm {{whole-milk}}, softened first {{unsalted-butter}} and the first {{eggs}}. Mix on low with a dough hook for 7 to 8 minutes; the dough should be soft, supple and pull cleanly from the sides of the bowl. Tip into an oiled bowl, cover, and bulk-ferment at warm room temperature for about 1 hour until doubled.

Beat the second {{unsalted-butter}}, {{soft-brown-sugar}} and {{cinnamon-ground}} to a soft paste. Knock back the dough and roll on a floured surface to a 60×30 cm rectangle, long edge in front. Spread the cinnamon paste evenly over. Fold the rectangle in thirds along the long axis (top down, bottom up) so you have a 60×10 cm three-layer strip. Cut crosswise into 16 strips, each 60×1 cm. For each strip: twist along its length 5 to 6 times, then wind around two fingers and tuck the tail through the loop. Set on lined trays with 4 cm between each.

Proof at warm room temperature for about 90 minutes until visibly puffed. Heat the oven to 220°C fan. Brush each bun with the beaten second {{eggs}} and scatter with {{demerara-sugar}}. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes; the buns should be deep gold and the cinnamon filling should be just visible at the edges. Cool 5 minutes on the tray before transferring to a rack. Best on the day they are baked.
